title: The new family story written into the wall of a house you own
subtitle: A five-minute guided visualisation for making the new money legacy feel solid and real.
excerpt: A guided visualisation for anchoring the new family money story as concrete and established. You see it written into the fabric of a home, proof that it existed, not aspiration that it might.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 14 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for the Day 14 theme: celebrating the new family story. The image is not a dream of what might be. It is a memory of what is already written.
, the grounding principles are the same.
Sit comfortably with your eyes closed. Take three slow breaths.
Picture a house you own. It doesn't have to be a house you own now. It can be any house that feels like yours. Solid walls. A door that opens when you push it.
Step inside. Walk through the rooms. Notice the light through the windows, the floor under your feet.

Find a wall, a good solid wall in a room that matters. Look closely at it. The new money story is written there. Not as graffiti or a poster. As something carved in: permanent, structural, part of the building. You can read what it says. Take it in.
This is the evidence. The story is not a wish. It is already in the fabric of the place.
Open your eyes when ready. Write one line about what you read if you want to keep it.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 14 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
